verses.text: A riddle: a great eagle takes the highest branch of the cedar and carries it to a land of merchants. Babylon and Judah. God speaks in parable to teach a hard lesson.
A riddle: a great eagle takes the highest branch of the cedar and carries it to a land of merchants. Babylon and Judah. God speaks in parable to teach a hard lesson.
A riddle: a great eagle takes the highest branch of the cedar and carries it to a land of merchants. Babylon and Judah. God speaks in parable to teach a hard lesson.
A riddle: a great eagle takes the highest branch of the cedar and carries it to a land of merchants. Babylon and Judah. God speaks in parable to teach a hard lesson.
He plants a seed that grows into a spreading vine, but it bends its roots toward a second great eagle. Divided loyalty. The vine that should have rested in its place reached after another power.
He plants a seed that grows into a spreading vine, but it bends its roots toward a second great eagle. Divided loyalty. The vine that should have rested in its place reached after another power.
He plants a seed that grows into a spreading vine, but it bends its roots toward a second great eagle. Divided loyalty. The vine that should have rested in its place reached after another power.
He plants a seed that grows into a spreading vine, but it bends its roots toward a second great eagle. Divided loyalty. The vine that should have rested in its place reached after another power.
Shall it prosper? Will not its roots be pulled up, to wither at the east wind? Folly exposed. What grows by treachery cannot stand.
Shall it prosper? Will not its roots be pulled up, to wither at the east wind? Folly exposed. What grows by treachery cannot stand.

The riddle is Babylon's king, who set Zedekiah on a humbled throne under oath, that the kingdom 'might be base' and not lift itself up. A covenant of submission. God had appointed even this lowly estate.

The riddle is Babylon's king, who set Zedekiah on a humbled throne under oath, that the kingdom 'might be base' and not lift itself up. A covenant of submission. God had appointed even this lowly estate.

The riddle is Babylon's king, who set Zedekiah on a humbled throne under oath, that the kingdom 'might be base' and not lift itself up. A covenant of submission. God had appointed even this lowly estate.

The riddle is Babylon's king, who set Zedekiah on a humbled throne under oath, that the kingdom 'might be base' and not lift itself up. A covenant of submission. God had appointed even this lowly estate.
But Zedekiah rebelled, sending to Egypt for horses; shall he prosper who breaks the covenant? Pharaoh will not save him in the war. Vain alliance. Help sought against God's will fails.
But Zedekiah rebelled, sending to Egypt for horses; shall he prosper who breaks the covenant? Pharaoh will not save him in the war. Vain alliance. Help sought against God's will fails.
But Zedekiah rebelled, sending to Egypt for horses; shall he prosper who breaks the covenant? Pharaoh will not save him in the war. Vain alliance. Help sought against God's will fails.
He despised the oath and broke the covenant; 'mine oath that he hath despised' God will repay on his head. Sworn faith matters. To break a solemn oath is to sin against God who witnessed it.
He despised the oath and broke the covenant; 'mine oath that he hath despised' God will repay on his head. Sworn faith matters. To break a solemn oath is to sin against God who witnessed it.
God will spread His net, bring him to Babylon, and plead with him there; his troops shall fall by the sword. No escape. The treaty-breaker meets the God whose name he scorned.
God will spread His net, bring him to Babylon, and plead with him there; his troops shall fall by the sword. No escape. The treaty-breaker meets the God whose name he scorned.

Yet God will take 'of the highest branch of the high cedar' and plant it on a lofty mountain, a goodly cedar sheltering every bird. The Messiah foretold. From the felled royal line God raises an everlasting kingdom.
